pattern: Counting numbers below a power of ten whose digits never repeat reduces to a length-by-length multiplication: the first digit has nine choices and each later digit has one fewer choice than the last.
hints: Group the numbers by how many digits they have, since a one-digit number can never clash with itself.
For a fixed digit length, the leading digit avoids zero giving nine options, and each subsequent position picks from the remaining unused digits.
Start the count at one for the number zero, then for each length from one up to n add nine times eight times seven down through the appropriate falling product, stopping once length exceeds ten.

edge_inputs.description: n is zero so only the single number zero counts
single digit, every value from zero to nine is unique
mid value where the falling product matters

stress.note: domain is tiny and fully enumerable
